facts: Stryten offers private label and Stryten branded storage products, services and solutions to the Essential Power and Motive industries.

Stryten Essential Power provides a suite of essential lead battery technologies and services, including chargers and racking systems, used for backup power in telecommunications, UPS, railway, utility and renewable industries.

Stryten Motive Power offers a comprehensive portfolio of motive power batteries, performance management tools and chargers at every price point – economy, mid-level and premium, including lithium technologies for material handling, mining, railway, military and other applications.
Stryten uses inCOMMAND software to factor fleet’s unique application needs into designed solutions.
M-Series Li510 Lithium battery enables 24/7 forklift operation using the same battery with only short bursts of recharging needed during worker breaks.
M-Series X-7 modular charger is configurable for standard, opportunity or fast charging and positively impacts productivity, uptime and management costs.
E-Series Absolyte GP battery features 2-volt battery cells proven for telecom, UPS, electric utility, rail and renewable energy, with a patented alloy that reduces corrosion.
Stryten Energy will share military-focused domestically manufactured energy storage solutions at DPEC 2026.
Stryten’s battery energy storage solutions are an ideal fit for critical government and military applications, strengthening operational readiness, resilience, and mission success.
Stryten Energy invested in future leaders with a summer intern program that welcomed 25 interns in 2025, with 18 working at headquarters in Alpharetta, GA.
Stryten Energy advanced veteran wellness by providing a generous financial donation to Rivers of Recovery for the sixth consecutive year.
The material handling industry is undergoing one of its fastest technology transitions in decades, with fleets modernizing around power, infrastructure and long-term trends.
The U.S. has designated lead as a critical mineral, underscoring the vital role recycled lead plays in domestic battery manufacturing, energy storage, and national security.
North America’s closed-loop lead-battery recycling system achieves high efficiency in the circular economy.
As demand for AI grows, power infrastructure for data centers is changing quickly, with utilities, commercial operations, and tech providers re-evaluating strategies.
Stryten Energy announced expanded capabilities of the M-Series X-7 charger at MODEX 2026, highlighting major updates that expand compatibility, power and flexibility.
A lead BESS installation at VICARS Community Center in Southwest Atlanta provides power resiliency to the community.
Stryten Energy launched a new M-Series Li610 lithium-ion battery in April 2026; it is U.S.-manufactured and extends material handling operations with longer battery cycle life.
Stryten Energy’s new Material Handling Battery Finder enables users to quickly match batteries to trucks, showcased at MODEX 2026.
Stryten Energy’s new E-Series AGM Multi-Terminal Batteries (AGM160/190) boost reliability for telecommunications and utility applications with a spill-proof design for harsh environments.
Stryten Energy’s mobile microgrid is designed for remote power and disaster relief, bringing power where it is most needed after storms cut access to the grid.
The Kansas House of Representatives issued House Resolution No. 6036 honoring Stryten’s 50 years of manufacturing at its Salina, Kansas plant.

Stryten Energy will showcase latest advancements to inCOMMAND energy performance management platform at MODEX 2026, featuring enhanced customization, faster performance and improved mobile usability for material handling dealers and end users.
As AI adoption accelerates across industries, its impact on energy demand within hyperscale data centers is becoming a critical infrastructure challenge.
At CES 2026, Stryten Energy convened experts to discuss the role of batteries in data center infrastructure.
Stryten Energy focuses on battery energy storage solutions built in the U.S. at NAATBatt 2026, noting that domestically sourced and manufactured systems can help solve increasing power demand.
Solar-plus-storage is gaining momentum but faces hurdles from software interoperability to supply chain constraints.
Stryten Energy highlights lead, lithium, and vanadium redox flow battery technologies designed for grid resilience and renewable energy integration at CES 2026.
Stryten’s scalable, tech-agnostic BESS solutions support data centers, manufacturing, and EV charging.
Distributed energy is redefining the power paradigm with renewable innovation and connectivity.
Stryten Energy showcased breakthrough battery technologies at AAPEX 2025, featuring Enhanced Flooded Battery technology and Battle Born lithium solutions built for next-gen performance.
OEMs are increasingly turning to Enhanced Flooded Batteries (EFBs) to meet the power needs of vehicles as vehicle technology evolves.

Stryten Energy powers resilience at BCI Flow Batteries North America with advanced energy storage solutions, delivering battery-first technologies needed to ensure uninterrupted power for commercial, industrial and utility-scale applications.
U.S. Senator Charles Grassley visited Stryten Energy’s transportation manufacturing plant in Manchester, Iowa, which recently celebrated 50 years of battery production.
Stryten Energy was named a 2025 Best Company for Women to Work by the Women’s Choice Award.

Georgia Tech and Stryten Energy unveiled an installation of a lead battery energy storage system for advanced research on April 23, 2025, to help Georgia Institute of Technology achieve carbon-neutral initiatives alongside existing solar.
Stryten Energy will feature BESS solutions at RE+ 2025, delivering resilient power for businesses facing outages and peak demand.
Stryten Energy welcomed U.S. legislators and staff to its manufacturing facilities across the country this summer.
Stryten Energy unveiled major performance and usability enhancements to inCOMMAND IoT platform on August 25, 2025, enabling more efficient fleet management.
The U.S. energy storage industry is navigating policy shifts, platform innovation, and evolving energy landscape demands to strengthen domestic supply chains and stay ahead of global disruption.
Stryten Energy will provide premium battery and charger solutions to KION’s North America dealer network, available through 200 sales and service locations nationwide.
The role of battery storage, particularly long-duration energy storage (LDES), is becoming increasingly critical in the quest for a sustainable energy future.
Selecting the right battery chemistry for material handling fleets involves performance, efficiency, longevity and safety considerations.
Stryten Energy’s Li600 material handling battery is now available from Hyster-Yale as a certified OEM option.
Safety in manufacturing environments is emphasized as a top priority year-round during National Safety Month.
Stryten Energy welcomed 25 summer interns in 2025, with 18 working at headquarters in Alpharetta, GA.
Support from Stryten Energy enables Rivers of Recovery to expand veteran rehabilitation programs through a generous financial donation for the fifth year.
Kansas Representative Steven K. Howe toured Stryten’s state-of-the-art manufacturing facility in Salina, Kansas.
Scott Childers serves as Vice President of Essential Power at Stryten Energy; Dr. Vick Singh is Chief Operating Officer at Dragonfly Energy.
Stryten Energy received the 2025 BCI Amplify Award for Product Marketing for its innovative video campaign for Enhanced Flooded Batteries.
Start-stop and hybrid vehicles demand more from batteries as electrical loads rise; EFBs offer a lower-cost alternative to AGM, with about 50% higher capacity and up to 52% higher cycle life.
Stryten Energy celebrates 50 years of manufacturing excellence in Salina, Kansas.
Georgia Tech and Stryten Energy unveiled installation of lead battery energy storage system for advanced research on April 23, 2025.
Lithium batteries are characterized by higher energy density, higher energy efficiency, a longer cycle life, and a longer calendar life compared to lead batteries.
China’s export ban on critical minerals, including antimony, gallium and germanium, effective December 3, 2024, is disrupting the battery manufacturing supply chain.

Stryten Energy unveiled a plan to add 10 Gigawatts of new U.S. energy storage manufacturing capacity on March 31, 2025; expansion will increase domestic supply of batteries for military, long-duration energy storage, transportation and material-handling applications.
Stryten Energy and Storion Energy teamed up to solve vanadium redox flow battery LDES cost barriers, delivering price-competitive vanadium electrolyte via a unique leasing model.
Stryten Energy received Rural King “Vendor of the Year” Award on March 13, 2025.
Power demand in the United States is projected to reach 4,185 billion kilowatt-hours (kWh) in 2025, a record high compared to 4,067 billion kWh in 2022.
Stryten Energy focuses on battery energy storage for U.S. energy resilience at NAATBatt 2025 using a technology-agnostic battery-first approach to meet increasing demand.
During CES 2025, Stryten Energy highlighted reimagining the future of energy resilience as the U.S. strives to meet skyrocketing energy demand while ensuring energy security and independence.

Storion Energy was launched to establish a domestic supply of flow battery components for long-duration energy storage; it is a Stryten Energy and Largo joint venture delivering price-competitive vanadium electrolyte via a unique leasing model.
The Inflation Reduction Act provided incentives for companies to invest in domestic manufacturing within the clean energy sector, including the Advanced Manufacturing Production Credit (AMPC).
Lead battery industry has dominated the U.S. landscape for over 100 years, but new challenges, technological shifts and increased competition demand attention.

Stryten Energy debuted a trailblazing mobile microgrid solution to advance U.S. energy resilience at CES 2025; the cutting-edge hybrid diesel-electric vehicle demonstrates a resilient energy ecosystem that efficiently manages energy sources, storage and usage.
Stryten Energy and Largo launched long-duration energy storage company Storion Energy on December 19, 2024.
Lithium batteries power everything from consumer electronics to electric cars and forklifts to backup power systems, and chemistry plays a role in performance, lifespan, cost and safety.
Stryten Energy was honored with four prestigious awards for marketing excellence in 2024.
Stryten Energy was named a 2025 Military Friendly Employer on November 26, 2024.
Batteries, disruption, and the age of electrification explores disruptions facing the lead battery industry driven by electrification, new battery technologies and economic and regulatory headwinds.
Retailers can meet consumers’ changing needs by re-examining battery inventory mix as vehicle power demands increase with start-stop technology, infotainment systems and advanced safety features.
Stryten Energy will feature latest innovations at AAPEX and the SEMA Show in Las Vegas on Nov. 4, 2024, showcasing full portfolio of advanced lead and lithium-ion transportation batteries.
Since the height of the pandemic when inflation peaked at 9.1 percent, there has been a downward trend with the annual inflation rate affecting battery prices.

Stryten Energy invested in REVKIT to expand product reach to automotive enthusiast community on October 15, 2024; will become preferred provider of advanced automotive batteries on REVKIT’s digital platform.
Battery energy storage systems (BESS) are necessary as more renewable energy sources come online and demand for energy continues to rise.

Stryten Energy was awarded DOE MAKE IT Prize Phase 2 funding for SAVES project to help rapidly scale US-based production and commercialization of cost-effective vanadium redox flow battery electrolyte on October 2, 2024.
Stryten Energy is sponsoring a team for the 2024 Rebelle Rally, an eight-day off-road challenge.
Lithium motive power batteries are becoming more prevalent in the motive power industry, with misconceptions about safety, cost and cold storage performance being dispelled.

Stryten Energy opened an expanded, state-of-the-art battery testing lab on August 28, 2024; facility is part of new corporate headquarters and includes dedicated space for testing lead, lithium and vanadium batteries and materials.
Comparing low-maintenance motive power battery options requires weighing several technologies to find the best fit for company fleets.
Jeremy Furr serves as Senior Vice President of Strategic Sourcing at Stryten Energy and emphasizes importance of domestic supply chain.

Stryten Energy inked a licensing agreement with Dragonfly Energy to launch Battle Born Batteries brand of lithium-ion batteries into new consumer retail markets; will have exclusive rights to market and distribute for military, automotive, marine, power sports, lawn & garden and golf cart applications.
Adding premium battery options helps consumers and increases automotive retail sales; offering EFB alongside AGM gives consumers a cost-effective option for start-stop and hybrid vehicles.
Stryten Energy won two top marketing awards in 2024 with creative partner Stakeholder Marketing and Media.
Stryten Energy received WITTDTJR Award from AutoZone on June 26, 2024.
Determining the right battery for motive power operations involves shift schedules, space utilization, power requirements, budget considerations.
Stryten Energy welcomed summer 2024 interns on June 12, 2024.
Mike Berger serves as Director of Product Engineering at Stryten Energy and discusses contributions of safe, proven and reliable lead batteries in transition to clean energy.
Stryten Energy expanded support of Rivers of Recovery on May 23, 2024.

Dan Autey serves as Executive Vice President of Commercial at Stryten Energy and explains how aging vehicles and expanding start-stop technology are increasing demand for advanced 12-volt batteries in automotive aftermarket.

Stryten Energy will showcase lead battery energy storage system technology at Battcon 2024 on May 13, 2024; lead BESS offers safe, proven and reliable solution for microgrids and EV-charging applications.
Advanced lead, lithium and vanadium battery chemistries will each play key roles in building more sustainable energy supply chains.
Stryten Energy was awarded MAKE IT Prize funding for SAVES project to build a domestic vanadium electrolyte manufacturing plant.

Stryten Energy was awarded Department of Defense funding for lithium-ion battery prototype on April 24, 2024; contract award supports DoD goals to strengthen domestic supply chain for battery technologies.

Stryten Energy is advancing Georgia’s clean energy industry with innovative battery technologies, helping to create a domestic supply chain that will contribute to job growth and economic development.
Automotive aftermarket needs category management services to help retailers source right mix of batteries.
Stryten Energy Motive Power Services deliver 24/7 support to minimize downtime and maximize motive power fleet performance.
